criteria.name: functional_correctness

Read AGENTS.md, docs/AGENT_HARNESS.md, docs/CODEX_AUTONOMY_POLICY.md, CODEX_TASKS.md, agent-state/progress.md, agent-state/feature_list.json, and agent-state/current_contract.json before evaluation.

Read F001-relevant licensing, test, repository structure, and ADR documents including docs/LICENSING.md, docs/TEST_STRATEGY.md, docs/REPOSITORY_STRUCTURE.md, docs/adr/0001-monorepo.md, docs/adr/0005-licence.md, and docs/adr/0006-long-running-agent-harness.md.

Required F001 legal and community files exist: LICENSE, NOTICE, ADDITIONAL_TERMS.md, TRADEMARKS.md, AUTHORS.md, THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, PR template, issue templates, branch-protection guidance, tooling files, scripts/test.ps1, scripts/test.sh, and CI workflow.
PowerShell and Git Bash init and smoke scripts passed and continued to report application scaffold checks as pending F002.
PowerShell and Git Bash canonical test scripts passed. Direct Python unit tests, npm test, and git diff --check passed.
Controlled temporary secret, JSON, and YAML fixtures failed as expected, were removed, and repository checks passed afterward.

criteria.name: product_depth
evidence: F001 is a repository-foundation increment; no product UI, API, provider adapter, database, or fake user workflow was introduced.
apps, packages, migrations, docker-compose.yml, and .env.example are absent, consistent with F001 scope and F002 being the application scaffold feature.

criteria.name: data_and_evidence_integrity
evidence: agent-state/feature_list.json still has F001 passes=false and no evaluator-created status change.
agent-state/current_contract.json parsed successfully and matches F001 scope.
Repository checks validate JSON, TOML, structural YAML, required files, license policy, dependency policy, secret patterns, script safety, formatting, and unsupported-claim guardrails.
Temporary malformed JSON and YAML fixtures produced actionable validation failures without corrupting active repository files.

criteria.name: zero_cost_safety
evidence: python tools/repo_checks.py all passed the unsupported-claims check.
No new provider records, quota values, pricing claims, region claims, Z0 classification logic, or public deployment proof was introduced.
Existing planning/product-language claims remain planning context rather than new F001 product evidence; F001 tooling guards new unsupported claims outside allowed planning documents.

criteria.name: security_privacy
evidence: Controlled fake secret fixture under .tmp/controlled-secret.txt was rejected by the secret scanner and removed.
Static search found no GitHub Actions secrets context, environment-dump commands, process.env usage, or shell environment printing in CI/scripts; matches found only in scanner patterns and tests.
CI workflow permissions are contents: read and it does not reference secrets.
.npmrc disables audit, funding, and update-notifier behavior for deterministic local checks without printing environment data.

criteria.name: portability
evidence: Repository checks passed in Windows PowerShell through scripts/test.ps1.
Repository checks passed in Git Bash through scripts/test.sh.
Tooling uses standard Python, Node, npm, Git, PowerShell, and Bash with no mandatory managed-cloud dependency.

criteria.name: code_quality
evidence: git diff --check passed.
Python unit tests passed: 3 tests run successfully.
npm test passed via node tools/node_check.mjs.
Pre-commit configuration delegates to repository-local Python and Node checks.
Dependency policy check passed and no runtime dependencies were added.

criteria.name: user_experience_and_accessibility
evidence: No public UI exists in F001. Contributor-facing docs provide concise local-check, PR, commit, evidence, and branch-protection guidance.
Issue templates request official source evidence and quota-exhaustion details without asserting publication.

criteria.name: regression_safety
evidence: Canonical F000 init and smoke paths still pass in PowerShell and Git Bash.
Application scaffold health remains explicitly pending F002.
No controlled temporary fixtures remained after negative checks; .tmp was empty.
Final git status showed only the builder's F001 workspace changes plus this evaluation file update.

NOTICE, ADDITIONAL_TERMS.md, TRADEMARKS.md, AUTHORS.md, THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md, pyproject.toml, and package.json consistently identify AGPL-3.0-only and required attribution/notice boundaries.

end_to_end_steps_run: Started from the current uncommitted F001 workspace and read all required harness, contract, ledger, licensing, test, repository, and ADR context.
Ran canonical initialization and smoke scripts through PowerShell and Git Bash; both passed and kept app scaffold checks pending F002.
Ran the canonical repository test command through PowerShell and Git Bash; both passed.
Ran direct Python unit tests, npm test, and git diff --check; all passed.
Created controlled ignored temporary fixtures for secret, malformed JSON, and malformed YAML validation; each failed as expected, was removed, and the full repository check suite passed afterward.
Inspected CI, pre-commit, branch-protection guidance, legal files, community templates, scripts, tooling, and absence of application scaffold paths.
recommendations: Builder may use this evaluation as evidence for marking F001 passing after normal end-of-session requirements are complete.
When opening the PR, mention that the CI controlled-failure gate is verified by the shared canonical check path and local temporary fixture probes, not by committing ignored .tmp fixtures.
